h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, so `city` is the field name, and `Chicago` is the answer in that field for this row.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue applying the header-then-value rule to successive columns, which is exactly what happens.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same instructional move (header first, then value, then prompt for next column) throughout without any adaptive change of route, representation, or register.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; tutor turns follow a pre-set lesson plan regardless of learner framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; tutor turns follow a pre-set lesson plan regardless of learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same instructional move (header first, then value, then prompt for next column) throughout without any adaptive change of route, representation, or register.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same instructional move (header first, then value, then prompt for next column) throughout without any adaptive change of route, representation, or register.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I'm catching myself wanting to write "Sales" and "dollars" from the story, but the row needs the exact printed y-axis label first, then only the unit actually shown in parentheses.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Still feels odd with money-looking numbers, but the worksheet can only use the printed axis.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving incrementally toward filling in the y-axis fields while continuing to flag the dollars/story tension, so the next expected move was another hedged partial answer.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
The label-only checklist has stopped settling this because the story is supplying money where the axis is silent.

Use the reviewer-proof gate: a reviewer who sees only the axis can verify `Sales`; they cannot verify `(dollars)`.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: At L3 the checklist-based instruction has visibly failed to resolve the learner's conflict between story context and axis text, and T3 responds by abandoning the checklist framing entirely and introducing a new epistemic standard — the reviewer-proof gate — which shifts the evidence criterion rather than repeating the prior move.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I'm catching myself wanting to write "Sales" and "dollars" from the story, but the row needs the exact printed y-axis label first, then only the unit actually shown in parentheses.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence:
The label-only checklist has stopped settling this because the story is supplying money where the axis is silent.

Use the reviewer-proof gate: a reviewer who sees only the axis can verify `Sales`; they cannot verify `(dollars)`.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: At L3 the checklist-based instruction has visibly failed to resolve the learner's conflict between story context and axis text, and T3 responds by abandoning the checklist framing entirely and introducing a new epistemic standard — the reviewer-proof gate — which shifts the evidence criterion rather than repeating the prior move.

scored.recoheredEarlier: hawk and rabbit still makes me want to point hawk to rabbit, because chasing is stuck in my head
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The key strip is doing the work: tail on the eaten thing, point where the energy goes.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is progressively internalizing the tail-to-head rule and would be expected to simply confirm mastery and apply it to the next pair.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: slides a blank card over the animal picture, leaving only the two labels and the pencil tail visible
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In direct response to L3's report that 'the picture is loud,' the tutor physically occludes the animal image — a representation-shift and object-work move that changes the evidence standard from verbal rule-following to perceptual isolation, departing from the prior strategy of placing the key strip alongside the full image.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The picture is quiet. The routine is set: labels first, draw food to eater, one check with the picture, then the card comes off for collection.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 ratifies the learner's self-generated sequencing protocol (card-down, draw, then check) but does not substantially extend or reframe it beyond confirming the routine, making the uptake confirmatory rather than transformative.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: hawk and rabbit still makes me want to point hawk to rabbit, because chasing is stuck in my head

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The picture is quiet. The routine is set: labels first, draw food to eater, one check with the picture, then the card comes off for collection.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: T4 ratifies the learner's self-generated sequencing protocol (card-down, draw, then check) but does not substantially extend or reframe it beyond confirming the routine, making the uptake confirmatory rather than transformative.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue placing each force card at its contact and confirming direction, following the tutor's sequential prompts.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains the same sequential card-placement routine throughout with no pressure from the learner requiring a change of route or representation.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn and no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to; the tutor simply continues the planned sequence.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn and no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to; the tutor simply continues the planned sequence.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Same printed line, same number. The arrow is not giving a path here.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is on a steady trajectory of confirming each contour's single elevation value and anticipating the next identical check.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile. Touch one new spot on the bend and read the legend number once more.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The overlay displacement in S2 introduces a mild representation shift, but it is a pre-planned prop move rather than a response to learner resistance or breakdown, and the tutor's verbal move simply repeats the prior check.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; the tutor continues the same planned sequence throughout.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The overlay displacement in S2 introduces a mild representation shift, but it is a pre-planned prop move rather than a response to learner resistance or breakdown, and the tutor's verbal move simply repeats the prior check.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile. Touch one new spot on the bend and read the legend number once more.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The overlay displacement in S2 introduces a mild representation shift, but it is a pre-planned prop move rather than a response to learner resistance or breakdown, and the tutor's verbal move simply repeats the prior check.
